title: Part 8 – Entraînement 3 : communication, santé & divertissement
instructions: Lisez les deux documents de chaque exercice et répondez aux questions.

passages.label: Conditions générales d'utilisation (extrait)
passages.text:
StreamLine Pro — Subscription Terms

Subscription Plans:
• Basic: €9.99/month — 1 screen, HD quality
• Standard: €14.99/month — 2 screens, Full HD quality
• Premium: €19.99/month — 4 screens, 4K quality

All plans include a 7-day free trial for new subscribers. Payment is charged on the first day after the trial ends. Subscribers may cancel at any time; cancellation takes effect at the end of the current billing period.

Family Sharing: Available on Standard and Premium plans. Each additional profile beyond the included screens incurs an extra €3/month.

Refund policy: No refunds are issued for partial months.

passages.label: E-mail d'un client
passages.text:
From: Oliver Jensen
To: support@streamlinepro.com
Subject: Billing Question

Hi,

I signed up for the Premium plan three weeks ago after my 7-day trial. I'd like to add two more screens so that my whole family (6 people) can use the service simultaneously.

I also want to check: if I decide to cancel next month, will I get a partial refund for the days I haven't used?

Finally, I noticed I was charged on October 8th — is that because my trial started on October 1st?

Thanks,
Oliver

questions.prompt: How many screens does the Premium plan include?
Oliver wants 6 simultaneous screens. How many extra screens does he need?
questions.explanation: Premium = 4 screens. Oliver needs 6. He needs 2 extra screens.

questions.prompt: What would be the extra cost for those additional screens?
Will Oliver receive a partial refund if he cancels mid-month?
choices: No, no refunds for partial months
Only if he cancels within 14 days
questions.explanation: "No refunds are issued for partial months".

questions.prompt: Why was Oliver charged on October 8th?
choices: He upgraded his plan on that date
questions.explanation: Trial started October 1st + 7 days = payment on October 8th (first day after trial).

passages.label: Brochure médicale
passages.text:
Sunrise Wellness Clinic — Corporate Health Packages

Package A — Basic Screening: €120/person
— Blood pressure and BMI measurement
— Cholesterol and glucose blood test
— Vision screening
— Written health report within 5 working days

Package B — Full Health Check: €220/person
— All Package A services
— ECG (heart rhythm)
— Lung function test
— Consultation with a GP (30 minutes)
— Written health report within 3 working days

Group discount: 10% for groups of 10 or more.
All screenings conducted on-site at your premises by appointment.
Minimum notice required: 2 weeks.

passages.label: E-mail d'une responsable RH
passages.text:
From: Claire Matos, HR Manager
To: Sunrise Wellness Clinic
Subject: Corporate Health Screening Enquiry

Dear Team,

We are interested in booking health screenings for our 45 employees. Most staff have expressed a preference for the Full Health Check, as they are particularly keen to have the GP consultation included.

Would the group discount apply to our booking? Also, our preferred date is March 7th — is that feasible given the two-week notice requirement?

Finally, could you confirm whether the Written health report would be ready within 3 working days for the Full Health Check?

We look forward to your response.
Claire Matos

questions.prompt: What is included in Package B but not Package A?
questions.explanation: "Package B — All Package A services plus: ECG, lung function test, GP consultation".

questions.prompt: How many employees does Claire's company have?
Will the group discount apply to their booking?
choices: Yes — they have more than 10 employees
Only if they choose Package A
questions.explanation: "Group discount: 10% for groups of 10 or more" — 45 employees qualifies.

questions.prompt: How long does a Package B report take?
questions.explanation: "Written health report within 3 working days" for Package B.

questions.prompt: Why does Claire mention March 7th specifically?
choices: It is the last day of the quarter
She wants to check if it meets the 2-week notice requirement
It was suggested by the clinic
questions.explanation: She asks "is that feasible given the two-week notice requirement?" — she is checking whether March 7th gives enough lead time.

passages.label: Programme du festival
passages.text:
URBAN ARTS FESTIVAL — Weekend Programme

Saturday:
10:00 a.m. — Street art workshops (all ages) — Central Plaza
1:00 p.m. — Live music: The Junction (indie/folk) — Main Stage
3:30 p.m. — Spoken word performance — Black Box Theatre
7:00 p.m. — Headline act: NOVA (electronic) — Main Stage

Sunday:
11:00 a.m. — Family art market — Riverside Park
2:00 p.m. — Film screening: 'City in Motion' (documentary) — Cinema Tent
5:00 p.m. — Closing ceremony and fireworks — Main Stage

Tickets: Day pass €25 / Weekend pass €40
Under-12s: Free
Food and drink vendors on site all weekend.

passages.label: E-mail entre amis
passages.text:
From: Petra
To: Marcus
Subject: Urban Arts Festival — this weekend!

Hi Marcus,

Are you free this weekend? I'm going to the Urban Arts Festival on Sunday with my kids (aged 8 and 11). We're planning to hit the art market in the morning and catch the documentary in the afternoon.

Ticket-wise, I'm getting a day pass for myself. The kids get in free, right?

Would you want to join us? If you can make Saturday too, the weekend pass saves you €10 compared to two day passes.

Let me know!
Petra

questions.prompt: What time is the headline act on Saturday?
questions.explanation: "7:00 p.m. — Headline act: NOVA (electronic) — Main Stage".

questions.prompt: Where will Petra and her kids go in the morning on Sunday?
questions.explanation: "Family art market — Riverside Park" at 11:00 a.m. Sunday.

choices: Yes, but at a reduced rate
Yes, weekend passes are required for children

questions.prompt: How much does a weekend pass cost?
How much does Marcus save by buying a weekend pass instead of two day passes?
questions.explanation: Two day passes = €25 × 2 = €50. Weekend pass = €40. Saving = €10.
